---
description: Generate accessible HTML report templates for open-scans, meeting WCAG 2.2 AA requirements.
---

## Context

You are creating or modifying an HTML report template for the **open-scans** scanner. Reports are generated by `scanner/interactive-report.mjs` and `scanner/generate-reports-html.mjs`, and are published to GitHub Pages. All report HTML must conform to **WCAG 2.2 Level AA**.

## Required ARIA Landmark Structure

Every report page must contain exactly these landmarks, in this order:

```html
<header role="banner">
  <!-- Site title and report metadata -->
</header>

<nav aria-label="Report navigation">
  <!-- Links to report sections or other scan results -->
</nav>

<main id="main-content">
  <!-- Primary report content -->
</main>

<footer role="contentinfo">
  <!-- Scan metadata, links, attribution -->
</footer>
```

Skip-navigation link before `<header>` (WCAG 2.4.1):

```html
<a class="skip-link" href="#main-content">Skip to main content</a>
```

## Accessible Table Patterns

All data tables require a `<caption>`, column headers with `scope`, and a `<thead>` / `<tbody>` split. Use `scope="col"` on column headers and `scope="row"` on row headers.

```html
<table>
  <caption>Accessibility violations by page — 42 issues found</caption>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th scope="col">Page URL</th>
      <th scope="col">Violations</th>
      <th scope="col">Impact</th>
      <th scope="col">WCAG SC</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <th scope="row"><a href="https://example.com/">Home</a></th>
      <td>12</td>
      <td>Critical</td>
      <td>1.4.3, 4.1.2</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
```

Never use `<table>` for layout. Never omit `<caption>` — it is the accessible name of the table (WCAG 1.3.1).

## Colour Contrast Requirements

All text must meet WCAG 1.4.3 (minimum contrast) and 1.4.11 (non-text contrast):

| Element | Minimum ratio |
|---|---|
| Normal text (< 18pt / < 14pt bold) | 4.5 : 1 |
| Large text (≥ 18pt / ≥ 14pt bold) | 3 : 1 |
| UI components, focus indicators | 3 : 1 |

Status indicators for severity levels must meet these ratios in **both** light and dark mode. Use CSS custom properties so a single colour palette change applies everywhere:

```css
:root {
  --color-critical: #d10000;   /* contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 on white */
  --color-serious:  #b35900;
  --color-moderate: #6b4f00;
  --color-minor:    #1a5e1a;
}

@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
  :root {
    --color-critical: #ff6b6b;  /* contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 on dark bg */
    --color-serious:  #ffaa55;
    --color-moderate: #ffe066;
    --color-minor:    #6ddc6d;
  }
}
```

The report uses `[data-theme="light"]` and `[data-theme="dark"]` overrides alongside `prefers-color-scheme`. Always provide all three: media query, light override, dark override.

## Focus Management

Every interactive element needs a visible focus indicator (WCAG 2.4.7, 2.4.11):

```css
:focus-visible {
  outline: 3px solid var(--color-focus, #005fcc);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

Do not suppress focus with `outline: none` or `outline: 0` without providing an alternative visible style.

For disclosure widgets (e.g., `<details>` / `<summary>` or custom expand/collapse), ensure keyboard users can operate them with Enter or Space (WCAG 2.1.1).

## Heading Hierarchy

Use a strict heading hierarchy — never skip levels (WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6):

```html
<h1>Scan report — 2026-03-27</h1>
  <h2>Summary</h2>
  <h2>Violations by page</h2>
    <h3>https://example.com/ — 12 violations</h3>
      <h4>colour-contrast (Serious)</h4>
      <h4>missing-alt (Critical)</h4>
  <h2>Cross-engine comparison</h2>
```

There must be exactly one `<h1>` per page.

## Screen Reader Announcement Patterns

Use `aria-live` regions only for content that changes dynamically after page load (WCAG 4.1.3). For static reports, avoid `aria-live` unless filtering or sorting updates content without a page reload:

```html
<!-- Status region for live filter feedback -->
<div role="status" aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true" id="filter-status">
  <!-- Updated by JS: "Showing 3 of 12 violations" -->
</div>
```

Use `role="status"` (polite) for non-urgent updates (filter counts, sort changes). Use `role="alert"` (assertive) only for errors that require immediate attention.

Do not use `aria-live` on large content areas — screen readers will read the entire region on every change.

## Interactive Elements

For filter controls and toggle buttons, always provide an accessible name and reflect state in ARIA attributes:

```html
<!-- Engine filter toggle -->
<button
  type="button"
  aria-pressed="false"
  aria-label="Filter by axe-core"
  class="engine-toggle"
  data-engine="axe"
>
  axe-core
</button>
```

Use `aria-pressed` (toggle) or `aria-expanded` (disclosure) — never both on the same element. Update these attributes in JavaScript when state changes.

## Dark Mode / Theme Toggle

The report's theme toggle button must persist user preference to `localStorage` under the key `report-theme`. The button must reflect the current theme in its accessible name:

```javascript
const themeToggle = document.getElementById("theme-toggle");
themeToggle.setAttribute("aria-label", `Switch to ${nextTheme} mode`);
```

## Rule Card Anchor Links

Each rule card uses a stable, slugified ID (`rule-{engine}-{ruleId}`) so that deep-linking to a specific finding is possible. Generate the slug with:

```javascript
function slugify(str) {
  return str.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-").replace(/(^-|-$)/g, "");
}
// → "rule-axe-color-contrast"
```

## stdout / stderr Convention

**Critical**: `generate-reports-html.mjs` and `interactive-report.mjs` write HTML to files — they do **not** use stdout for HTML output. Progress messages go to **stderr**; structured summaries (JSON) go to **stdout**.

## Checklist Before Submitting

- [ ] `<a class="skip-link" href="#main-content">` present before `<header>`
- [ ] All four landmark regions present: `banner`, `nav`, `main`, `contentinfo`
- [ ] All `<table>` elements have `<caption>`, `scope` attributes, `<thead>`/`<tbody>`
- [ ] Colour contrast ratios verified for light and dark mode
- [ ] CSS custom properties used for all status indicator colours
- [ ] `[data-theme="light"]` and `[data-theme="dark"]` overrides present alongside `prefers-color-scheme`
- [ ] Focus indicators visible in both light and dark mode (min 3:1 contrast)
- [ ] Exactly one `<h1>` per page; heading hierarchy has no skipped levels
- [ ] `aria-live` regions used only for dynamically-updated content
- [ ] `aria-pressed` or `aria-expanded` used and updated for interactive controls
- [ ] Theme toggle persists to `localStorage` as `report-theme`
- [ ] Rule card IDs use `rule-{engine}-{ruleId}` slug format
- [ ] `npm test` passes
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes
